User Story: As a CLI user, I want running benchmark with no arguments to show help so that I can discover commands.
Expected Behavior: When sys.argv has only the program name, main() calls parser.print_help() and returns exit code 0 without running analysis.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want --version to print version info so that I can confirm the installed build.
Expected Behavior: Top-level --version prints: 'Privacy-Compliant Peer Benchmark Tool', 'Version: 3.0.0', Python version, and platform; returns exit code 0.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want help text to list available presets so that I can choose one without a separate command.
Expected Behavior: create_parser() epilog includes EXAMPLES and get_presets_help() output listing preset names and descriptions when PresetManager loads successfully.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want config list to enumerate presets so that I can see available configurations.
Expected Behavior: benchmark config list prints preset_mgr.format_preset_list() (names, descriptions, compliance posture, key optimization settings) and returns exit code 0.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want config show PRESET to display full preset YAML so that I can inspect settings.
Expected Behavior: benchmark config show <preset_name> prints format_preset_detail() including version, description, compliance_posture, full YAML, and usage example; returns 0. If preset missing, prints not-found message listing available presets.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want config validate to check a YAML file so that I know it is schema-valid.
Expected Behavior: On success prints '[OK] Configuration file is valid: <path>' and returns 0. On failure prints '[FAIL] Configuration validation failed:' plus errors and returns 1.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want config generate to copy the template so that I can start a custom config.
Expected Behavior: Copies config/template.yaml to output path if template exists and output does not exist; prints '[OK] Configuration template created: <path>' plus edit/validate hints; returns 0. Returns 1 if template missing, output exists, or copy fails.

User Story: As a CLI user, I want running config without a subcommand to show usage so that I know valid config commands.
Expected Behavior: If config_command is unset, prints 'Usage: benchmark config {list|show|validate|generate}' and subcommand descriptions; returns exit code 1.

User Story: As an analyst, I want the share subcommand so that I can run volume-distribution benchmarking.
Expected Behavior: share parser registers share-only flags plus add_common_run_flags; main routes args.command=='share' to run_share_analysis after setup_logging.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel --preset balanced_default

User Story: As an analyst, I want the rate subcommand so that I can run approval/fraud rate benchmarking.
Expected Behavior: rate parser registers rate-only flags plus add_common_run_flags; main routes args.command=='rate' to run_rate_analysis after setup_logging.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py rate --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --total-col total --approved-col approved --fraud-col fraud --dimensions card_type --preset balanced_default

User Story: As an analyst, I want --csv to specify input data so that the run loads my dataset.
Expected Behavior: --csv is required on share and rate; value passed through AnalysisRunRequest.csv and loaded via DataLoader in prepare_run_data unless args.df is preloaded.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel --preset balanced_default

User Story: As a remote user, I want reduced Textual animation overhead so that the TUI performs well over SSH/VPN.
Expected Behavior: Before Textual import, os.environ.setdefault sets TEXTUAL_SMOOTH_SCROLL=0, TEXTUAL_ANIMATIONS=none, TEXTUAL_FPS=20 (overridable via environment).
Verification Method: manual: set TEXTUAL_ANIMATIONS=default and relaunch to compare; defaults are set at import

User Story: As a power user, I want documented keyboard shortcuts so that I can drive the TUI without the mouse.
Expected Behavior: BINDINGS: ctrl+o Open CSV, ctrl+r Run, f1 Preset Guide, ctrl+a Advanced, ctrl+e Export Adv, ctrl+l Clear Log; on_mount notify lists these for 6 seconds.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, read startup notification and footer binding labels

User Story: As an analyst, I want to type a CSV path so that I can point the tool at my dataset.
Expected Behavior: Input #csv_path placeholder "path/to/data.csv" in section_data; initial focus on mount; Enter (on_input_submitted) or blur (on_input_blurred) triggers _try_load_csv_from_path_input.

User Story: As an analyst, I want a Browse button so that I can pick a file without typing the full path.
Expected Behavior: Button #btn_browse_csv label "Browse…" calls action_open_file (same as Ctrl+O).
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, click Browse… and confirm CsvPickerScreen opens

User Story: As an analyst, I want quick picks from common folders so that I can load fixture data fast.

Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, Browse…, select a quick-pick CSV and confirm path fills #csv_path

User Story: As an analyst, I want to browse the workspace tree so that I can open CSVs outside quick picks.
Expected Behavior: CsvDirectoryTree #picker_tree rooted at os.getcwd(); filter_paths keeps dirs and .csv files, hides dot dirs and __pycache__; FileSelected dismisses modal with path.

Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, Browse…, press Escape and confirm path unchanged

User Story: As an analyst, I want invalid paths rejected so that I do not proceed with missing files.

Feature: CSV header load and column populate

User Story: As an analyst, I want a compact file summary so that I can verify the loaded dataset.

User Story: As an analyst, I want an optional time-period column so that time-aware consistency can be enabled.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to specify an output path so that reports land where I expect.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to choose an optimization preset so that solver behavior matches my intent.

User Story: As an analyst, I want the preset description shown inline so that I understand the selected preset without opening help.

Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, switch presets and read blurb under selector

User Story: As an analyst, I want a preset guide button so that I can read preset intents without leaving the app.
Expected Behavior: Button #btn_preset_help and F1/action_show_help push PresetHelpScreen modal with Markdown descriptions for six shipped presets.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to toggle impact/distortion analysis so that reports include or omit impact sheets.

User Story: As an analyst, I want optional preset comparison so that I can evaluate distortion across presets.
Expected Behavior: Checkbox #compare_presets default False; passed as compare_presets in run values.

User Story: As an analyst, I want input validation before execution so that data issues are surfaced early.

User Story: As an analyst, I want optional calculated metric columns in CSV export so that I can inspect raw vs balanced values.
Expected Behavior: Checkbox #include_calculated label "Include calc. metrics (CSV)" default False; passed as include_calculated.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, enable and run with export balanced CSV

User Story: As an analyst, I want to explicitly acknowledge accuracy-first posture so that non-compliant runs are intentional.
Expected Behavior: Checkbox #acknowledge_accuracy_first default False; when preset compliance_posture is accuracy_first and box unchecked, _fail_launch blocks run with focus on checkbox.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, select accuracy_first preset without checking box and attempt run

User Story: As an analyst, I want to choose analysis vs publication output so that I control report redaction level.
Expected Behavior: Select #output_format options Analysis/publication/both values analysis/publication/both; default analysis; allow_blank=False; passed as output_format.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, change Output format and run

User Story: As an analyst, I want advanced optimization parameters hidden by default so that the form stays approachable.
Expected Behavior: Collapsible #advanced_opt title "Advanced Optimization Parameters" collapsed=True; Ctrl+A action_toggle_advanced toggles collapsed and refreshes preset values when expanded.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, press Ctrl+A to expand/collapse advanced section

User Story: As an analyst, I want to override LP tolerance and iteration settings so that I can tune optimization beyond presets.
Expected Behavior: Inputs adv_lp_tolerance, adv_lp_max_iterations, adv_lp_lambda_penalty, adv_lp_volume_weighting_exponent and checkbox adv_lp_volume_weighted_penalties populated from preset via ADVANCED_FIELD_SPECS on preset change/expand.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to override volume preservation and weight bounds so that I can customize constraint strength.
Expected Behavior: Inputs adv_constraints_volume_preservation, adv_bounds_min_weight, adv_bounds_max_weight under Advanced section.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to configure subset search behavior so that infeasible dimensions can be handled per my policy.
Expected Behavior: Checkbox adv_subset_enabled; inputs adv_subset_strategy, adv_subset_max_attempts, adv_subset_max_slack_threshold; checkboxes adv_subset_trigger_on_slack, adv_subset_prefer_slacks_first.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to tune Bayesian fallback parameters so that LP failures use my preferred heuristic settings.
Expected Behavior: Inputs adv_bayes_max_iterations, adv_bayes_learning_rate under "Bayesian Optimization (Fallback)".
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, expand Advanced, inspect Bayesian fields

User Story: As an analyst, I want to override BIC percentile and debug output flags so that analysis and workbook content match my needs.
Expected Behavior: Input adv_analysis_bic_percentile; checkboxes adv_output_debug_sheets, adv_output_privacy_validation.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to apply advanced values as a temporary config file so that the next run uses my overrides atop the preset.
Expected Behavior: Button #btn_apply_advanced calls apply_advanced_overrides: collects ADVANCED_FIELD_SPECS via ConfigOverrideBuilder, writes override YAML via PresetWorkflow.write_override_file, stores path in advanced_config_path, notifies success/failure.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, edit advanced field, click Apply Overrides, confirm notification

User Story: As an analyst, I want to export advanced overrides to a persistent YAML file so that I can reuse settings outside the TUI.
Expected Behavior: Button #btn_export_advanced and Ctrl+E action_export_advanced expand advanced if collapsed, apply overrides, then export_override_file with timestamped name; notifies export path.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, Ctrl+E or Export Config and confirm export notification

User Story: As an analyst, I want a Share Analysis tab so that I can configure share-based benchmarking.
Expected Behavior: TabPane #share_tab is initial active tab in TabbedContent; contains share_metric, share_secondary, dimension options, share_dims SelectionList.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to choose the primary share metric column so that share analysis uses the correct numerator.
Expected Behavior: Select #share_metric required for share mode; blank triggers _fail_launch "Primary metric is required"; changing metric calls update_secondary_options excluding primary from #share_secondary.

User Story: As an analyst, I want optional secondary share metrics so that additional columns appear in output.
Expected Behavior: SelectionList #share_secondary class multi-select;height 7; options all CSV columns except primary metric; selected list passed as secondary_metrics.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, Share tab, select secondary metrics

User Story: As an analyst, I want auto-detect dimensions so that I do not have to pick every dimension manually.
Expected Behavior: Checkbox #share_auto_dim default False; when True hides #share_dims and #share_dims_label via hidden class; auto=True passes dimensions=None.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, toggle Share auto-detect and observe dimension list hide/show

Feature: Share debug and CSV export toggles
User Story: As an analyst, I want share-specific debug and CSV export options so that I control share run artifacts.
Expected Behavior: #share_debug default True→debug; #share_export_csv default True→export_balanced_csv; per_dimension_weights hardcoded False for share.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to pick share dimensions manually when auto-detect is off so that analysis scope is explicit.
Expected Behavior: SelectionList #share_dims; when auto off, at least one dimension required or _fail_launch "Select at least one dimension or enable auto-detect" focus share_dims.

User Story: As an analyst, I want a Rate Analysis tab so that I can configure approval/fraud rate benchmarking.
Expected Behavior: TabPane #rate_tab contains rate_total, rate_approved, rate_fraud, rate_secondary, dimension options, rate_dims; _analysis_mode returns rate when active tab is rate_tab.

Expected Behavior: Selects #rate_total (required), #rate_approved and #rate_fraud allow_blank=True; run requires total_col and at least one of approved_col or fraud_col.

Expected Behavior: SelectionList #rate_secondary; update_secondary_options excludes #rate_total when total changes.

User Story: As an analyst, I want rate-specific dimension, debug, export, and BPS options so that rate runs match my reporting needs.
Expected Behavior: #rate_auto_dim hides #rate_dims when on; #rate_debug default True; #rate_export_csv default False; #fraud_in_bps default True passed as fraud_in_bps.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, Rate tab, toggle Fraud in BPS and export options

User Story: As an analyst, I want the status panel mode line to reflect the active tab so that I know which analysis type will run.
Expected Behavior: on_tabbed_content_tab_activated calls _refresh_run_status; mode line shows share or rate from _analysis_mode.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, switch Share/Rate tabs and read Run Status mode line

User Story: As an analyst, I want a prominent Run button so that I can start analysis from the form.
Expected Behavior: Button #btn_run label "▶  Run Analysis" variant primary; press or Ctrl+R calls run_analysis; disabled and relabeled "Running…" during run.

User Story: As an analyst, I want missing CSV blocked before execution so that runs do not start without data.
Expected Behavior: If #csv_path empty, _fail_launch "CSV path is required." writes ERROR to log, error notify, _reset_run_ui, focuses csv_path.

Feature: Run preflight share metric and dimensions
User Story: As an analyst, I want share-mode field validation so that incomplete share configuration is rejected before backend work.
Expected Behavior: Share mode requires share_metric; requires auto-detect or at least one share_dims selection; failures use _fail_launch with focus on offending widget.

User Story: As an analyst, I want rate-mode field validation so that rate runs have required numerator/denominator columns.
Expected Behavior: Rate mode requires rate_total; requires rate_approved and/or rate_fraud; dimension rule same as share with rate_auto_dim/rate_dims.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, Rate tab, clear required columns and attempt run

User Story: As an analyst, I want a second run click ignored while one is active so that concurrent runs do not corrupt state.
Expected Behavior: run_analysis returns immediately if #btn_run.disabled and not confirmed/saved_request path.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, start run and click Run again while Running…

User Story: As an analyst, I want analysis on a worker thread so that the UI stays responsive during long runs.
Expected Behavior: @work(thread=True) on run_analysis; UI updates via call_from_thread for _begin_run_ui, _end_run_ui, push_screen, log writes.

User Story: As an analyst, I want live run status so that I can see readiness, mode, preset, and elapsed time.
Expected Behavior: Static #run_status border_title Run Status; _STATE_BADGES idle/ready, running/yellow, success/green, error/blocked red; shows mode, preset, elapsed seconds every 1s while running via set_interval.

User Story: As an analyst, I want a last-run summary so that I can read verdict and report paths without scrolling the log.
Expected Behavior: Static #results_panel starts "No runs yet."; _end_run_ui updates with state badge, elapsed timestamp, mode/preset, compliance verdict color, posture, report paths.

User Story: As an analyst, I want a scrolling execution log so that I can follow analysis progress and errors.
Expected Behavior: Log #log_output highlight=False max_lines=2000 border_title Execution Log; cleared at run start; receives LogHandler output and explicit write_log_message lines.

User Story: As an analyst, I want to clear the execution log so that I can reduce noise between runs.
Expected Behavior: Ctrl+L action_clear_log calls #log_output.clear().
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, populate log, press Ctrl+L

User Story: As an analyst, I want Python logging mirrored in the TUI log so that backend messages appear in the UI.
Expected Behavior: setup_logging_capture attaches LogHandler to root logger INFO with timestamp formatter; dedupes benchmark/core handlers; on_unmount removes LogHandler instances.

User Story: As an analyst, I want log updates from worker threads without UI corruption so that long runs log reliably.
Expected Behavior: LogHandler batches records and flushes via call_from_thread when off app thread; write_log_message uses same pattern.

User Story: As an analyst, I want validation issues listed in a modal so that I can review errors and warnings before proceeding.
Expected Behavior: ValidationModal shows error/warning counts, ListView #issue_list with severity-labeled messages and optional row previews; Proceed disabled when any ERROR present.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, run with validate_input on problematic data

User Story: As an analyst, I want to proceed past warnings so that non-fatal issues do not block my run.
Expected Behavior: Proceed button dismisses True; callback reruns run_analysis(confirmed=True, saved_request, saved_df) when no errors and not should_abort; otherwise cancels and _reset_run_ui.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, trigger warnings-only validation and click Proceed

User Story: As an analyst, I want to cancel after validation so that I can fix data without executing.
Expected Behavior: Cancel button or Escape action_cancel_validation dismisses False; logs "Analysis cancelled by user." and _reset_run_ui.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, open validation modal and Cancel or Escape

User Story: As an analyst, I want a disk log file per run so that I can audit execution outside the TUI.
Expected Behavior: Each run creates outputs/logs/benchmark_log_{timestamp}.txt via setup_logging console_output=False; path written to execution log.

User Story: As an analyst, I want a completion toast with report path so that I know where output was saved.
Expected Behavior: On success, notify "Report saved: {first path}" title Analysis Complete information 10s plus results_panel summary with fully_compliant green markup when applicable.

User Story: As an analyst, I want blocked and failed runs surfaced clearly so that I can diagnose compliance or execution problems.
Expected Behavior: RunBlocked→_end_run_ui blocked state and error notify; RunAborted/other exceptions→error state, log traceback, results_panel red message.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, provoke blocked run if possible

User Story: As an analyst, I want quit blocked with confirmation during a run so that I do not accidentally abort analysis.
Expected Behavior: action_quit when _run_state==running pushes ConfirmScreen "An analysis is still running. Quit anyway?" confirm_label Quit; confirmed saves session and exit(); otherwise no-op.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, start run, attempt quit and confirm/cancel dialog

Feature: Session save on run and quit
User Story: As a returning user, I want my form state saved so that I can resume configuration later.
Expected Behavior: SESSION_FILE=~/.benchmark_tui/session.yaml; _save_session on _begin_run_ui and action_quit; yaml.safe_dump of inputs, selects (SELECT_BLANK→null), checkboxes, SelectionList selections.

User Story: As a returning user, I want prior session restored on launch so that I do not re-enter common fields.
Expected Behavior: _restore_session on_mount reads YAML; _apply_session loads csv if file exists, restores entity_col then entity_name, other selects/checkboxes/selection lists skipping invalid stale values.

User Story: As a developer/tester, I want blank Select values detected reliably across Textual versions so that optional fields and session restore behave consistently.
Expected Behavior: SELECT_BLANK=getattr(Select,NULL,getattr(Select,BLANK,None)); compared when reading select values for session save, run assembly, and entity/time blank handling.

User Story: As an analyst, I want warned when using best_effort posture so that I understand outputs may be non-compliant.
Expected Behavior: When preset compliance_posture is best_effort, run logs WARNING line to execution log before execute_run.
Verification Method: manual: launch py tui_app.py, select best_effort preset preset and run

User Story: As an analyst, I want clarity that lean and audit_package are not in the TUI so that I use CLI for those options.
Expected Behavior: TUI_UNSUPPORTED_FIELDS documents df, prepared_dataset, lean, audit_package, validate_export, report_format, control3_overrides have no widgets; not exposed in form.

User Story: As an analyst I want privacy weights optimized globally so peer concentration stays within Control 3.2 caps across all dimension categories.
Expected Behavior: When consistent_weights=True, GlobalWeightOptimizer.solve_full_problem() calls LPSolver.solve(build_lp_request(...)); SciPy linprog tries methods in order highs, highs-ds, highs-ipm; on success sets analyzer.last_lp_stats and returns peer→multiplier dict.

User Story: As a developer I want to know the LP decision vector so I can interpret solver diagnostics and slack stats.
Expected Behavior: Variables are [m₀…mₚ₋₁, t⁺₀…t⁺ₚ₋₁, t⁻₀…t⁻ₚ₋₁, s_cap₀…s_cap_{C×P−1}, s_rank₀…s_rank_{K−1}] where P=peers, C=unique (dimension,category) vectors, K=rank-pair count; objective minimizes t⁺+t⁻ plus weighted s_cap and rank_preservation_strength×s_rank.
Notes: Stats keys num_vars and num_constraints written to last_lp_stats

User Story: As an analyst I need each peer's weighted share in every category to respect the selected privacy cap.
Expected Behavior: For each category vector v and peer p: (1−cap)·v_p·m_p − cap·Σ_{j≠p} v_j·m_j − s_cap ≤ 0 with cap=max_concentration/100; one inequality per peer per category.

User Story: As an analyst I want weights to stay near 1.0 unless caps force adjustment.
Expected Behavior: For each peer p: m_p − t⁺_p ≤ 1 and −m_p − t⁻_p ≤ −1 encode |m_p−1| via t⁺, t⁻ in objective with unit coefficients.

User Story: As an analyst I want peer volume ranking preserved where possible after balancing.
Expected Behavior: Rank pairs (i,j) with base_shares[i]≥base_shares[j] get constraint peer_vol[i]·m_i − peer_vol[j]·m_j + s_rank_k ≤ 0; s_rank penalized by rank_preservation_strength (mapped from volume_preservation_strength, default 0.5).

User Story: As a developer I can enforce rank ordering between all higher-vs-lower baseline-share peer pairs.
Expected Behavior: When rank_constraint_mode='all' (default), pair_indices includes every (i,j) with base_shares[i]≥base_shares[j] in descending order.

User Story: As a developer I can limit rank constraints to k nearest lower-ranked neighbors per peer.
Expected Behavior: When rank_constraint_mode='neighbor', only pairs (order[a], order[b]) for b in [a+1, min(P,a+1+rank_k)) are added; rank_constraint_k default 1.
Verification Method: Manual: build DimensionalAnalyzer(rank_constraint_mode='neighbor', rank_constraint_k=2) and inspect LP num_constraints via debug run

User Story: As an analyst I need peer multipliers bounded to prevent extreme distortion.
Expected Behavior: Each m_p bounded [min_weight, max_weight]; defaults min_weight=0.01, max_weight=10.0 from DimensionalAnalyzer and SolverRequest.

User Story: As an analyst I expect final multipliers to average ~1.0 while respecting bounds.
Expected Behavior: After linprog, LPSolver rescales m by k=min(max(1/mean, min_w/min m), max_w/max m) then clips to [min_weight,max_weight]; _store_final_weights repeats similar rescale before persisting global_weights.

User Story: As an analyst running high-volume presets I want cap violations in large categories penalized more heavily.
Expected Behavior: When volume_weighted_penalties=True, each s_cap coefficient is base_lambda_cap × (cat_vol/total_cat_vol)^volume_weighting_exponent; stats['volume_weighted']=True.

User Story: As a developer I can tune how aggressively volume scales slack penalties.
Expected Behavior: volume_weighting_exponent (default 1.0) is the exponent on category volume share when computing per-cap slack penalties.

User Story: As a developer I can override the LP slack penalty scale via lambda_penalty.
Expected Behavior: If lambda_penalty is not None, base_lambda_cap=max(lambda_penalty,0); otherwise base_lambda_cap=100.0/max(tolerance,1e-6).

User Story: As an analyst I can allow small cap excess via tolerance percentage points in LP and validation.
Expected Behavior: LP uses tolerance only for lambda_cap scaling; post-solve _is_share_violation treats adjusted_share > max_concentration+tolerance+COMPARISON_EPSILON as violation; default tolerance=1.0 pp.

User Story: As a developer I can cap SciPy linprog iterations via max_iterations.
Expected Behavior: Positive max_iterations passed to linprog options as maxiter; non-positive values ignored.

User Story: As a developer I need solver telemetry for audit and subset-search decisions.
Expected Behavior: Successful LP writes last_lp_stats with method, max_slack, sum_slack (pct of category volume), residual_cap_violation, num_vars, num_constraints, lambda_cap, etc.

User Story: As an analyst I want one privacy weight set applied across all dimensions when dashboards require consistency.
Expected Behavior: When consistent_weights=True, calculate_global_privacy_weights() builds categories for all dimensions, solves globally, sets weight_methods[dim]='Global-LP' for used dims; returns WeightingResult.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel --preset balanced_default --debug

User Story: As an analyst I can optimize weights independently per dimension when global consistency is disabled.
Expected Behavior: When consistent_weights=False, calculate_global_privacy_weights returns None; fit_privacy_weights calls _solve_per_dimension_weights for each dimension without global solve.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type --per-dimension-weights

User Story: As a developer I call one method to fit weights regardless of consistency mode.
Expected Behavior: fit_privacy_weights(df, metric_col, dimensions) delegates to calculate_global_privacy_weights when consistent_weights else per-dimension _solve_per_dimension_weights; returns weighting_result_from_analyzer snapshot.

User Story: As a developer I construct DimensionalAnalyzer from merged ResolvedConfig without manual kwarg wiring.
Expected Behavior: DimensionalAnalyzer.from_resolved(resolved, ...) maps bounds, LP, subset_search, bayesian, dynamic_constraints, merchant_mode, rank_constraints, enforce_single_weight_set onto constructor.

User Story: As an analyst I must not receive identity weights when peer count cannot satisfy any privacy rule.
Expected Behavior: build_weighting_problem raises ValueError when privacy rule is 'insufficient'; sets compliance_blocked_reason='insufficient_peers' and clears weight_methods.

User Story: As an analyst I must not get unvalidated weights when LP, subset, and heuristic all fail.
Expected Behavior: run_heuristic_fallback raises ValueError with compliance_blocked_reason='optimization_failed' if heuristic_solver.solve returns None.

User Story: As an analyst I want subset search when LP succeeds but uses excessive cap slack.
Expected Behavior: When trigger_subset_on_slack=True and sum_slack from last_lp_stats exceeds max_cap_slack+COMPARISON_EPSILON via _is_slack_excess, subset search may replace full-set LP solution.

User Story: As an analyst I configure how much aggregate LP slack triggers subset search.
Expected Behavior: max_cap_slack (default 0.0) compared against stats sum_slack (% of category volume); excess rejects subset candidate or triggers slack-aware policy.

User Story: As an analyst I want the largest feasible dimension set found by dropping the most unbalanced dimension iteratively.
Expected Behavior: When greedy_subset_search=True (default), repeatedly LP-tests trial_dims, scores dimensions via _dimension_unbalance_scores, drops max-score dim until success or max_tests.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel --preset compliance_strict --debug

User Story: As an analyst I can explore random dimension subsets when greedy search is disabled.
Expected Behavior: When greedy_subset_search=False, iterates subset sizes n−1…1, shuffles combinations with random.Random(0) for reproducibility, returns first feasible low-slack subset.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel --preset balanced_default --debug

User Story: As a developer I cap how many subset LP trials run per analysis.
Expected Behavior: subset_search_max_tests (default 200) bounds greedy loop and random combination tests; minimum 1 attempt enforced.

User Story: As an analyst I need an audit trail of subset search attempts in reports.
Expected Behavior: Each attempt appends dict to analyzer.subset_search_results with Attempt, Dimensions, Count, Success, Max_Slack, Sum_Slack, Method, Note; surfaced in Subset Search Excel sheet.

Feature: Auto subset search on infeasible LP
User Story: As an analyst I want automatic dimension dropping when global LP is infeasible.
Expected Behavior: When lp_solution is None and auto_subset_search=True and not single_weight_mode, calls search_largest_feasible_subset then _solve_per_dimension_weights on removed dims.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel --preset balanced_default --debug

User Story: As an analyst I can retry full-dimension LP with rank preservation disabled before dropping dimensions.
Expected Behavior: When prefer_slacks_first=True and initial LP fails, temporarily sets rank_preservation_strength=0.0 and re-runs LP before other fallbacks.

User Story: As an analyst I get a deterministic fallback when auto_subset_search is off and LP is infeasible.
Expected Behavior: Without auto_subset_search, drops dimensions in descending _dimension_unbalance_scores order one-at-a-time until LP succeeds, then per-dimension solves removed dims.

User Story: As an analyst I still get valid weights on dimensions excluded from the global solve.
Expected Behavior: _solve_per_dimension_weights runs per-dimension LP on removed_dimensions; on success sets per_dimension_weights[dim] and weight_methods[dim]='Per-Dimension-LP'.

User Story: As an analyst I need dimensions that still violate caps after global weights to get their own multipliers.
Expected Behavior: post_validate_and_correct identifies violating user dimensions (excluding internal _TIME_*), solves per-dim LP with tolerance=0 temporarily, else Per-Dimension-Bayesian.

User Story: As an analyst I get a best-effort weight set when per-dimension LP fails.
Expected Behavior: HeuristicSolver L-BFGS-B via build_heuristic_request; weight_methods[dim]='Per-Dimension-Bayesian'; strict tolerance anchors target_weights=None else global weights.

User Story: As an analyst I still receive weights when global LP and subset search both fail.
Expected Behavior: run_heuristic_fallback sets all weight_methods to 'Global-Bayesian', uses full problem categories, sets heuristic_converged from stats.

User Story: As an analyst I see which solver produced weights per dimension in Weight Methods sheet.
Expected Behavior: weight_methods values: Global-LP, Per-Dimension-LP, Per-Dimension-Bayesian, or Global-Bayesian; get_weights_dataframe exposes Scope/Method columns.

User Story: As an analyst I can forbid per-dimension fallback to preserve one global multiplier set.
Expected Behavior: enforce_single_weight_set=True skips slack-triggered subset search, dimension-drop fallbacks, and post-validation per-dimension reweighting; violations logged but global weights kept.

User Story: As an analyst on 6/30, 7/35, or 10/40 rules I need tier participant requirements checked after LP.
Expected Behavior: finalize_converged_weights calls _find_additional_constraint_violations per category when enforce_additional_constraints and rule in (6/30,7/35,10/40); populates additional_constraint_violations list.

User Story: As an analyst I want heuristic optimization to reduce tier violations after LP.
Expected Behavior: When violations found, runs HeuristicSolver with target_weights=None if tolerance≈0 else current global weights; re-checks violations afterward.

User Story: As an analyst analyzing sparse buckets I can relax tier thresholds based on representativeness.
Expected Behavior: When dynamic_constraints_enabled=True, HeuristicSolver._assess_additional_constraints_applicability may skip or scale thresholds using min_peer_count, effective_peers, volume_share, representativeness floors; dynamic_constraint_stats tracked.

User Story: As an analyst I want tiny cap excesses corrected without full re-solve.
Expected Behavior: _nudge_borderline_cap_excess scales worst violator weight when max excess ≤ BORDERLINE_CAP_EXCESS_TOLERANCE_PP (1e-3 pp) and within min/max bounds.
Verification Method: Manual: inspect post-LP weights on near-feasible fixture

User Story: As an analyst with a time column I need one weight set satisfying caps for every month and month×category slice.
Expected Behavior: When time_column set and consistent_weights=True, CategoryBuilder.build_time_aware_categories adds _TIME_TOTAL_{col} monthly totals and {dim}_{col} time-suffixed categories; GlobalWeightOptimizer uses them in global LP.

User Story: As an analyst I should not fail when configured time column is absent from CSV.
Expected Behavior: If time_column not in df.columns, CategoryBuilder logs warning and falls back to build_categories without time constraints.

User Story: As a developer I cannot use dimension names colliding with internal time constraint prefixes.
Expected Behavior: CategoryBuilder.validate_dimension_names raises ValueError if any dimension starts with reserved prefix _TIME_.

User Story: As an analyst benchmarking a target entity, privacy weights apply only to peers.
Expected Behavior: CategoryBuilder excludes target_entity from peer_df when building categories; peer list derived from non-target entities only.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type --debug

User Story: As an analyst I can run peer benchmarks without a target entity.
Expected Behavior: target_entity=None: share/rate metrics omit Target Share/Rate and Distance columns; impact requires explicit target_entity arg; categories include all entities as peers.

User Story: As an analyst I need upfront warning when caps are impossible without weighting.
Expected Behavior: build_weighting_problem calls _compute_structural_caps_diagnostics; get_structural_infeasibility_summary exposes infeasible_dimensions, worst_margin_pp, top_infeasible_category.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel --debug
Notes: Structural Detail sheet when LP fails

User Story: As a developer I read final peer multipliers and rank changes from analyzer state.
Expected Behavior: _store_final_weights writes global_weights[peer] with multiplier, unbalanced/balanced volume and share, legacy volume/weight; builds rank_changes_df with Base_Rank, Adjusted_Rank, Delta.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type --debug

User Story: As a developer I export global and per-dimension multipliers for debugging.
Expected Behavior: get_weights_dataframe returns rows with Scope Global/Per-Dimension, Method, Peer, Multiplier, volume/share columns.

User Story: As a developer applying weights in impact math, per-dimension multipliers override global.
Expected Behavior: WeightLookup.multiplier(peer, dim) returns per_dimension_weights[dim][peer] if present else global_weights[peer].multiplier else 1.0; used by impact calculators.

User Story: As an analyst I want balanced peer average share and BIC per category for each dimension.
Expected Behavior: analyze_dimension_share → AnalysisCalculator groups by entity×dimension(×time), calls _calculate_share_metrics producing Balanced Peer Average (%), BIC (%), optional Target Share and Distance.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type channel

User Story: As an analyst I want balanced peer approval/fraud rates per category.
Expected Behavior: analyze_dimension_rate aggregates total and numerator columns, _calculate_rate_metrics computes weighted balanced rate and BIC on peer rates.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py rate --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --total-col total --approved-col approved --fraud-col fraud --dimensions card_type

User Story: As an analyst with time_col I see per-period and General rollup rows per category.
Expected Behavior: When time_column in df, AnalysisCalculator emits one row per (category, time_period) plus a General aggregate row per category.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type --time-col year_month

User Story: As an analyst I benchmark against a high-performing peer percentile rather than the mean.
Expected Behavior: bic_percentile default 0.85; _weighted_percentile computes weighted percentile of peer category shares (share) or rates (rate) using peer volume×multiplier weights.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type

User Story: As an analyst in debug mode I see unweighted peer averages and impact vs balanced.
Expected Behavior: debug_mode=True adds Original Peer Average (%), volume totals, Impact (pp) to share/rate results; enables weights tracking logs.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type --debug

User Story: As an analyst I need target share change (pp) from privacy weighting by dimension/category.
Expected Behavior: calculate_share_impact computes Raw_Share_% vs Balanced_Share_% using peer multipliers; Impact_PP=balanced−raw; supports time periods; requires target entity.

User Story: As an analyst I need peer rate change (pp) from privacy weighting excluding target from peer pool.
Expected Behavior: calculate_rate_impact weights peer numerators/denominators by dim multipliers; outputs {rate}_Raw_%, {rate}_Balanced_%, {rate}_Impact_PP per dimension/category/time.

User Story: As an analyst I want roll-up distortion stats overall, by dimension, and by time.
Expected Behavior: calculate_impact_summary groups Impact_PP (or rate *_Impact_PP) into mean/min/max/std rows for Overall, By Dimension, By Time Period.

User Story: As a developer on legacy scripts I can still call distortion/weight-effect method names.
Expected Behavior: calculate_share_distortion wraps calculate_share_impact and adds Distortion_PP=Impact_PP; calculate_rate_weight_effect adds *_Weight_Effect_PP; calculate_distortion_summary warns and delegates to calculate_impact_summary; removal planned v4.0.

User Story: As a developer I can attach extra metric column names on the analyzer for downstream CSV export.
Expected Behavior: DimensionalAnalyzer.secondary_metrics: Optional[List[str]] initialized None; set by run orchestration before balanced export (not used inside optimization solvers).

User Story: As a developer I receive structured compliance state with global optimization.
Expected Behavior: assemble_weighting_result sets WeightingComplianceState verdict strict_compliant|best_effort|non_compliant from residual_cap_violation, additional violations, relaxation_used, heuristic_converged.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want 5-peer groups capped at 25% per participant so that Control 3.2 5/25 is enforced.
Expected Behavior: Rule 5/25: min_entities=5, max_concentration=25.0%; evaluate_rule fails primary_cap_passed when any balanced share exceeds 25.0%+COMPARISON_EPSILON (1e-6); no secondary tier requirements.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want 6-peer groups capped at 30% with at least three participants at 7%+ so that 6/30 is enforced.
Expected Behavior: Rule 6/30: min_entities=6, max_concentration=30.0%; secondary tier_1 requires >=3 participants with share+1e-6>=7.0%; failure message format 'Need 3 participants >= 7%, found N'.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want 7-peer groups capped at 35% with two >=15% and one additional >=8% so that 7/35 is enforced.
Expected Behavior: Rule 7/35: min_entities=7, max_concentration=35.0%; secondary tiers normalized to (2,15.0) and cumulative (3,8.0) meaning >=2 at 15%+ and >=3 total at 8%+.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want 10-peer groups capped at 40% with two >=20% and one additional >=10% so that 10/40 is enforced.
Expected Behavior: Rule 10/40: min_entities=10, max_concentration=40.0%; secondary tiers (2,20.0) and cumulative (3,10.0); participant_count_passed false when <10 peers with message 'Need at least 10 participants, found N'.

User Story: As a merchant benchmarker, I want 4-peer merchant groups capped at 35% so that merchant-only 4/35 applies.
Expected Behavior: Rule 4/35: min_entities=4, max_concentration=35.0%; no additional secondary requirements; select_rule returns 4/35 only when merchant_mode=True and peer_count equals 4.

User Story: As an analyst, I want shares exactly at the cap to pass and tiny excess beyond epsilon to fail so that floating-point comparisons are stable.
Expected Behavior: primary_cap_passed when share <= max_concentration+COMPARISON_EPSILON (1e-6); share at cap+1e-8 passes; share at cap+0.001 fails with primary_cap_failures=1 for all standard rules.

User Story: As an analyst, I want secondary participant thresholds to use the same epsilon-inclusive comparison so that 6.999% does not count as >=7%.
Expected Behavior: _count_at_or_above counts value when value+COMPARISON_EPSILON>=threshold; 6/30 fails at [6.999,6.999,6.999,...]; passes at [7.0,7.0,7.0,...].

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want each rule to reject groups below its min_entities so that under-sized peer sets cannot pass.
Expected Behavior: participant_count_passed false when len(shares)<min_entities; e.g. 5/25 with 4 peers fails even if shares within cap; message 'Rule {name}: Need at least {min} participants, found {n}'.

User Story: As an analyst, I want peer_count>=10 to auto-apply the 10/40 rule so that the strictest applicable cap is chosen.
Expected Behavior: PrivacyValidator.select_rule(peer_count) sorts rules by min_entities descending (excluding 4/35) and returns first rule where peer_count>=min_entities; peer_count=10 returns 10/40.

User Story: As an analyst, I want 7-9 peers to auto-apply 7/35 so that mid-size peer groups use the correct cap.
Expected Behavior: select_rule(7) returns 7/35; same rule applies for peer_count 7,8,9 because 10/40 requires min_entities=10.
Verification Method: py -c "from core.privacy_validator import PrivacyValidator; assert PrivacyValidator.select_rule(8)=='7/35'"
Notes: No dedicated pytest; inline assert documents 8-peer selection.

User Story: As an analyst, I want exactly 6 peers to auto-apply 6/30 so that the correct cap and secondary tiers are used.
Expected Behavior: select_rule(6) returns 6/30 with max_concentration=30.0 and additional min_count_above_threshold [3,7.0].
Verification Method: py -c "from core.privacy_validator import PrivacyValidator; assert PrivacyValidator.select_rule(6)=='6/30'"

User Story: As an analyst, I want exactly 5 peers to auto-apply 5/25 so that the minimum standard issuer rule is used.
Expected Behavior: select_rule(5) returns 5/25 with max_concentration=25.0 and no additional constraints.
Verification Method: py -c "from core.privacy_validator import PrivacyValidator; assert PrivacyValidator.select_rule(5)=='5/25'"

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want fewer than 5 standard peers to yield rule 'insufficient' so that analysis cannot proceed with identity weights.
Expected Behavior: select_rule returns 'insufficient' when peer_count<5 (non-merchant); global_weight_optimizer raises ValueError and sets compliance_blocked_reason='insufficient_peers' with message 'Minimum 5 peers required for standard mode (4 for merchant mode)'.

User Story: As an analyst, I want peer concentration computed as entity metric sum divided by total times 100 so that shares are percentages.
Expected Behavior: calculate_concentration and _build_concentration_frame group by entity_column summing metric, then concentration=(metric/total)*100; zero total yields concentration=0 or warning 'Total for metric ... is zero'.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want validate_peer_group to fail fast when entity count is below min_participants so that undersized groups are rejected before cap checks.
Expected Behavior: Returns (False, ['Insufficient entities: {n} < {min}']) immediately when unique entities in entity_column < min_participants; does not evaluate concentration.

User Story: As an analyst, I want cap violations to name the entity, metric, cap, and observed share so that remediation is actionable.
Expected Behavior: Warning format: "Entity '{name}' exceeds {max_concentration}% for '{metric}': {concentration:.2f}%"; validate_peer_group returns is_compliant=False.

User Story: As an analyst, I want metrics with zero total to emit a warning but not alone fail compliance so that optional zero columns do not block valid metrics.
Expected Behavior: When metric total==0, append warning 'Total for metric ... is zero', skip cap check (continue), and do not set is_compliant=False for that metric alone.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want configured protected entities capped at 25% by default (Citi Control 3) even under looser peer rules so that protected participants have stricter limits.
Expected Behavior: When protected_entities non-empty and protected_max_concentration omitted, PROTECTED_ENTITY_DEFAULT_MAX_CONCENTRATION=25.0 applies; e.g. rule 10/40 still caps protected entity at 25%.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want to override protected-entity concentration limits so that institution-specific caps can be enforced.
Expected Behavior: Protected entity exceeding protected_max_concentration fails with warning "Protected entity '{name}' exceeds {cap}% for '{metric}': {share:.2f}%" even when share is within general max_concentration.

User Story: As an analyst, I want apply_weighting to iteratively reduce dominant peer values to the threshold so that concentration can be brought within cap.
Expected Behavior: Iterates up to 10 times; threshold_decimal=(threshold_percentage/100)-0.0001; sets adjustment_factor=max_allowed/original for capped peers; post-weighting max concentration<=threshold.

User Story: As an analyst, I want validate_fallback_rules to try 5/25 then 6/30 then 7/35 then 10/40 in order so that the most permissive passing rule can be identified.
Expected Behavior: Iterates rule_order=['5/25','6/30','7/35','10/40']; returns first compliant rule_name; if none pass returns (None, False, ['All privacy rules failed validation']).
Verification Method: Manual: instantiate PrivacyValidator and call validate_fallback_rules on test DataFrame
Notes: No dedicated pytest in listed suite.

User Story: As an operator, I want privacy thresholds loaded from config/privacy_rules.yaml (or PBT_PRIVACY_RULES_FILE) so that rules can be externalized without code changes.
Expected Behavior: DEFAULT_RULES_FILE=config/privacy_rules.yaml; PBT_PRIVACY_RULES_FILE env overrides path; reload_rules validates schema (positive min_entities, positive max_concentration) and caches in _RULES_CACHE; falls back to DEFAULT_RULES on load failure.

User Story: As an optimizer, I want get_penalty_thresholds to expose secondary tiers for LP penalties so that optimization matches validation.
Expected Behavior: Maps 6/30 to tier_1:(3,7.0); 7/35 to tier_1:(2,15.0), tier_2:(1,8.0); 10/40 to tier_1:(2,20.0), tier_2:(1,10.0); empty dict for rules without additional.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want additional 6/30/7/35/10/40 secondary rules enforced by default so that full Control 3.2 tiers apply during optimization.
Expected Behavior: Config default optimization.constraints.enforce_additional_constraints=true; PrivacyPolicySettings.enforce_additional_constraints default False at class level but DimensionalAnalyzer.from_resolved_config sets from merged config (default True).
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type --preset compliance_strict

User Story: As an analyst, I want to disable additional secondary enforcement so that only primary caps are checked when configured.
Expected Behavior: assess_additional_constraints returns ConstraintDecision(enforce=False, reason='additional constraints disabled') when enforce_additional_constraints=False.
Verification Method: Config override enforce_additional_constraints:false then inspect Privacy Validation sheet Additional_Constraints_Enforced=No

User Story: As an analyst, I want additional tiers skipped when the selected rule is 'insufficient' so that invalid peer counts do not attempt tier checks.
Expected Behavior: assess_additional_constraints returns enforce=False with reason "rule 'insufficient' has no additional constraints".
Verification Method: Blocked before validation when insufficient_peers; see D-013

User Story: As an analyst, I want additional tiers skipped when category peer count is below min_peer_count_for_constraints so that sparse buckets are not over-penalized.
Expected Behavior: assess_additional_constraints returns enforce=False with reason 'peer count {n} below minimum {min_peer_count_for_constraints}' (default min_peer_count=4 from config dynamic_constraints, PrivacyPolicySettings default 6 if unset).

User Story: As an analyst, I want dynamic_constraints enabled to scale tier counts and thresholds by representativeness so that sparse categories get relaxed secondary requirements.
Expected Behavior: When dynamic_constraints_enabled=True: scale=max(threshold_scale_floor, min(1.0, representativeness/min_representativeness)); adj_count=max(1, round(count*count_scale)); adj_threshold=threshold*scale; relaxed=True when adjusted differs from base.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want relaxed dynamic thresholds to never count as strict_passed so that compliance summary flags them.
Expected Behavior: evaluate_rule with mode=ADAPTIVE and relaxation_used=True yields strict_passed=False even when secondary_rule_passed=True; additional_constraints_result ignores participant failures in non-STRICT mode.

User Story: As an optimizer, I want heuristic solver to skip additional constraints for low effective peers, low volume, or low representativeness when dynamic mode is on so that unrepresentative buckets are not over-constrained.
Expected Behavior: heuristic_solver._assess_additional_constraints_applicability returns reasons: disabled, no_additional, time_total, low_peers, low_effective_peers, low_volume, low_representativeness based on min_peer_count (default 4), min_effective_peer_count 3.0, min_category_volume_share 0.001, min_overall_volume_share 0.0005, min_representativeness 0.1.
Verification Method: Inspect dynamic_constraint_stats counters after run with dynamic_constraints.enabled:true

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want fraud or chargeback rate runs blocked unless privacy_basis is clearing_spend so that issuer fraud benchmarking uses the required basis.
Expected Behavior: evaluate_control3_policy: when rate_types intersect {fraud, chargeback} and normalized privacy_basis != 'clearing_spend', allowed=False, blocked_reason='fraud_chargeback_requires_clearing_spend_basis'.

User Story: As an analyst, I want explicit clearing_spend basis to allow fraud/chargeback runs so that compliant runs can proceed.
Expected Behavior: When privacy_basis='clearing_spend' (normalizes hyphens/spaces to underscores) and rate_types includes fraud, allowed=True and requirements['fraud_chargeback_privacy_basis']='enforced'.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want runs declaring digital wallet metrics blocked until digital_wallet_review_approved is true so that Control 3.4 review is evidenced.
Expected Behavior: contains_digital_wallet_metrics=True and digital_wallet_review_approved=False -> blocked_reason='digital_wallet_metrics_require_privacy_review'.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want any run declaring top-merchant output hard-blocked so that prohibited deliverables cannot be produced.
Expected Behavior: contains_top_merchant_output=True always returns allowed=False with blocked_reason='top_merchant_lists_not_allowed' regardless of other approvals.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want dual-entity-axis benchmarks blocked until dual_entity_axis_review_approved is true so that both protected axes are reviewed.
Expected Behavior: dual_entity_axis=True and dual_entity_axis_review_approved=False -> blocked_reason='dual_entity_axis_requires_privacy_review'; digital wallet approval does not satisfy this gate.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want recurring deliverables blocked when last_privacy_recheck_date is missing or older than 365 days so that annual re-check is enforced.
Expected Behavior: recurring_deliverable=True: needs_recheck when last_privacy_recheck_date is None or (today-recheck_date).days>365 -> blocked_reason='recurring_deliverable_recheck_required'.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want peer_group_altered recurring runs blocked unless last_privacy_recheck_date equals today so that peer changes trigger immediate re-check.
Expected Behavior: When recurring_deliverable=True and peer_group_altered=True and recheck_date!=today, needs_recheck=True even if within 365 days.

User Story: As an auditor, I want allowed runs to record which Control 3 gates were enforced or not applicable so that compliance evidence is traceable.
Expected Behavior: On allow, Control3PolicyResult.requirements includes keys like control_3_2_numeric_rules='enforced_by_privacy_validator', digital_wallet_review='not_applicable' or 'manual_approval_recorded', recurring_recheck_evidence status, etc.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want accuracy_first runs blocked without explicit acknowledgement so that non-strict posture is intentional.
Expected Behavior: enforce_compliance_preconditions: posture=='accuracy_first' and not acknowledge_accuracy_first raises RunBlocked with message 'accuracy_first compliance_posture requires explicit acknowledgement before execution' and compliance_verdict='blocked', acknowledgement_state='required_missing'.

User Story: As a developer, I want RunBlocked to carry a serializable compliance_summary so that CLI/TUI can report block reason without partial outputs.
Expected Behavior: RunBlocked(message, compliance_summary dict) subclasses RunAborted; used for accuracy_first ack, Control 3 policy blocks, insufficient_peers optimization abort.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want strict posture to report run_status='non_compliant' when violations>0 and 'compliant' when clean so that strict runs are binary.
Expected Behavior: ComplianceSummary.to_dict: posture=='strict' -> run_status 'non_compliant' if violations>0 else 'compliant'; posture_consistent=False when strict and violations>0.

User Story: As an analyst, I want best_effort posture to complete with warnings when violations exist so that exploratory runs can finish.
Expected Behavior: posture=='best_effort' -> run_status 'completed_with_warnings' if violations>0 else 'compliant'.
Verification Method: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type --compliance-posture best_effort

User Story: As an analyst, I want accuracy_first posture to always report run_status='completed_accuracy_first' when not blocked so that fidelity-priority runs are labeled distinctly.
Expected Behavior: posture=='accuracy_first' (with acknowledgement) -> run_status='completed_accuracy_first' regardless of violation count.

User Story: As an auditor, I want compliance_verdict='fully_compliant' only when no violations and no structural infeasibility so that clean runs are identifiable.
Expected Behavior: compliance_verdict='fully_compliant' when violations==0 and not has_structural_infeasibility and input_verdict!='not_publishable_input'.

User Story: As an auditor, I want compliance_verdict='violations_detected' when strict final validation finds cap, participant, secondary, or relaxed-row issues so that non-compliance is explicit.
Expected Behavior: violations>0 -> compliance_verdict='violations_detected'; strict_final_validation.total_violations sums primary_cap_fail_rows + participant_count_fail_categories + secondary_rule_fail_categories + relaxed_rows.

User Story: As an auditor, I want compliance_verdict='structural_infeasibility' when no violations but structural infeasibility is present so that impossible categories are distinguished from cap breaches.
Expected Behavior: When violations==0 and structural_infeasibility.has_structural_infeasibility=True -> compliance_verdict='structural_infeasibility'.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want strict runs with non-publishable input data flagged as not_publishable_input so that bad inputs cannot appear compliant.
Expected Behavior: When posture=='strict' and data_quality.publishable is False, details.input_verdict='not_publishable_input' and violations incremented by 1.

User Story: As an operator, I want blocked runs to serialize run_status and compliance_verdict as 'blocked' so that pre-analysis gates are distinguishable from completed runs.
Expected Behavior: When details.blocked=True: run_status='blocked', compliance_verdict='blocked', acknowledgement_state='acknowledged' or 'required_missing', posture_consistent=False.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want build_strict_final_validation to re-evaluate canonical rules per dimension/category group so that row-level 'Compliant=Yes' labels cannot hide violations.
Expected Behavior: Groups by Dimension+Category(+Time_Period); calls evaluate_rule on Balanced_Share_% list; counts relaxed Additional_Constraints_Relaxed='Yes' rows as violations even if Compliant='Yes'.

User Story: As a compliance officer, I want publication outputs withheld when strict posture has violations so that non-compliant peer evidence is not published.
Expected Behavior: write_outputs sets block_publication when posture=='strict' and violations>0; metadata publication_withheld_reason='strict_posture_violations'.
Notes: Test uses best_effort; publication redaction is separate Control 3.3 behavior.

User Story: As an auditor, I want each privacy validation row marked strict_compliant only when primary cap passes, secondary passes, and no relaxation was used so that row-level strict status is unambiguous.
Expected Behavior: strict_compliant = primary_cap_passed and secondary_rule_passed and not relaxation_used; primary_cap_passed uses analyzer._is_share_violation (share > cap + tolerance + epsilon).

User Story: As an analyst, I want the Privacy Validation sheet/CSV to expose cap, tolerance, constraint detail, and structural margins so that audits are self-contained.
Expected Behavior: to_dataframe emits Compliant, Primary_Cap_Passed, Additional_Constraints_Enforced/Relaxed/Passed, Additional_Constraint_Detail, Structural_Infeasible_Peer/Category, Violation_Margin_%, Strict_Compliant, etc.

User Story: As an analyst, I want _TIME_TOTAL_ dimension rows to validate primary caps only so that monthly totals are not subject to per-category secondary tiers.
Expected Behavior: append_time_total_rows sets secondary_rule_enforced=False, secondary_rule_passed=True, secondary_rule_detail='Time total cap validation'.

User Story: As an analyst, I want input validation to error when peer count is below minimum (5 standard, 4 merchant) so that insufficient groups are caught early.
Expected Behavior: validate_share_input ERROR insufficient_peers when peer_count < thresholds.min_peer_count (default 5); merchant_mode lowers threshold to min(...,4).

User Story: As an operator, I want CLI flags for privacy_basis, digital wallet, top merchant, dual axis, and recurring deliverable declarations so that policy gates can be satisfied or triggered explicitly.
Expected Behavior: benchmark.py flags: --privacy-basis {clearing_spend,transaction_count,transaction_amount}; --contains-digital-wallet-metrics; --digital-wallet-review-approved; --contains-top-merchant-output; --dual-entity-axis; --dual-entity-axis-review-approved; --recurring-deliverable; --last-privacy-recheck-date; --peer-group-altered mapped to control3 config.

User Story: As a config author, I want only strict, best_effort, and accuracy_first accepted as compliance_posture so that invalid postures are rejected at load time.
Expected Behavior: VALID_COMPLIANCE_POSTURES=frozenset({'strict','best_effort','accuracy_first'}); ConfigValidator and ConfigManager._validate_compliance_posture reject others.
Notes: Uses config validation context; posture enum enforced similarly.

User Story: As a user loading CSV data, I want column headers normalized so CLI flags match consistently.
Expected Behavior: Headers lowercased; spaces/dots/dashes become underscores; non-alphanumeric stripped; repeated underscores collapsed; collisions append _{index} with WARNING log.

User Story: As a user, I want a clear error when the CSV path does not exist.
Expected Behavior: load_from_csv raises FileNotFoundError with message 'CSV file not found: {path}'.
Verification Method: CLI: py benchmark.py share --csv /nonexistent.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --dimensions card_type

User Story: As an operator, I want oversized CSV files rejected before load.
Expected Behavior: When input.max_csv_size_mb is set and file exceeds it, raises ValueError citing actual MB and configured limit.

User Story: As an operator, I want row caps enforced during CSV read.
Expected Behavior: Respects nrows arg or input.max_csv_rows; chunked reads truncate at limit.

User Story: As a user with explicit dimensions, I want only analysis-relevant columns loaded.
Expected Behavior: When project_csv_columns=True and dimensions explicit (no --auto), usecols resolves entity_col, dimensions, time_col, metric/secondary/total/approved/fraud; missing requested cols logged as warning.

User Story: As a user on large CSVs, I want chunked pre-aggregation when safe.
Expected Behavior: When adaptive_batching, explicit dimensions, validate_input disabled, and row/file thresholds exceeded: reads chunks, groupby-sums group_columns+value_columns, compacts partials; last_workload_estimate.should_batch=True.

User Story: As a user with input validation on, I want row-level data preserved for checks.
Expected Behavior: Adaptive batching not enabled when validate_input_enabled=True even if size thresholds met; reason notes 'input validation enabled'.

User Story: As a user, I want the tool to detect count+amount+entity columns heuristically.
Expected Behavior: validate_minimal_schema returns True when _is_count_like, _is_amount_like, and _is_entity_like columns all present; mapped mode uses column_mapping roles when schema_detection_mode is mapped/hybrid.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want approved+total/declined+entity columns detected.
Expected Behavior: validate_full_schema True when approved-like plus (total-like OR declined-like) plus entity-like columns exist.
Verification Method: Manual: inspect logs on rate fixture CSV

User Story: As a user, I want schema classified as full, minimal, or unknown.
Expected Behavior: detect_schema_type returns 'full' if full schema, else 'minimal' if minimal, else 'unknown'.

User Story: As a user with --auto, I want categorical dimension candidates listed.
Expected Behavior: get_available_dimensions excludes numeric columns and identifier keyword columns (entity, issuer, merchant, id, code, name).
Verification Method: CLI: py benchmark.py share --csv tests/fixtures/gate_demo.csv --entity Target --metric txn_cnt --auto

User Story: As the engine, I want NA filled and all-zero metric rows removed before analysis.
Expected Behavior: preprocess_data fills NA (default 0) and drops rows where all numeric columns are zero when remove_zeros=True.

User Story: As a TUI/CLI user, I want structured validation findings with severity and optional fixes.
Expected Behavior: ValidationIssue dataclass fields: severity (ERROR/WARNING/INFO), category, message, row_indices, auto_fix_available, fix_description, details; __str__ formats [SEVERITY] category: message.

User Story: As a share analyst, I want missing required columns flagged before run.
Expected Behavior: validate_share_input ERROR category missing_columns listing metric, entity, dimensions, time_col; returns early without further checks.

User Story: As a share analyst, I want nulls in critical columns surfaced.
Expected Behavior: Null pct > max_null_percentage (default 5%) => ERROR null_values with row_indices; smaller null counts => WARNING.

User Story: As a share analyst, I want negative metric values blocked.
Expected Behavior: Any metric_col < 0 => ERROR negative_values with up to 50 row_indices.

User Story: As a compliance user, I want insufficient peers flagged.
Expected Behavior: peer_count < min_peer_count (default 5) => ERROR insufficient_peers.

User Story: As a target-entity analyst, I want missing or case-mismatched entities reported.
Expected Behavior: Missing target => ERROR missing_entity with up to 10 available entity samples; case-insensitive match => INFO entity_case.

User Story: As a share analyst, I want sparse dimension categories warned.
Expected Behavior: Per dimension, categories with < min_rows_per_category (default 3) rows => WARNING small_categories listing up to 5 category names.

User Story: As a share analyst, I want dominant entities warned.
Expected Behavior: Entity share of total metric > max_entity_concentration (default 50%) => WARNING high_concentration with entity:share% dict.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want missing numerator columns blocked.
Expected Behavior: Empty numerator_cols => ERROR missing_columns 'No numerator columns provided'; missing total/entity/numerator/dimension/time cols => ERROR missing_columns.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want numerator>denominator rows flagged.
Expected Behavior: num_col > total_col (+1e-9 epsilon) => ERROR invalid_rate per rate name with row_indices.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want unstable low-denominator rows warned with context.
Expected Behavior: 0 < total < min_denominator (default 100) => WARNING low_denominator with dimension category breakdown in message and details.affected_categories.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want rates above 100% treated as errors.
Expected Behavior: Computed rate > 100% + epsilon => ERROR invalid_rates; uses RATE_COMPARISON_EPSILON for float tolerance at exactly 100%.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want extreme out-of-range rates warned.
Expected Behavior: Rates outside 0-100% beyond max_rate_deviation (default 50pp) => WARNING outlier_rates.

User Story: As an analyst, I want a Summary sheet with run metadata and compliance posture.
Expected Behavior: Sheet 'Summary' rows: Analysis Type, Entity, Timestamp, Dimensions, Privacy Rule (participants/max%), Compliance Posture/Verdict/Acknowledgement/Run Status, Primary Cap, Secondary Rule, Relaxation Used, Strict Final Validation, Input Validation, Metrics Analyzed list.

User Story: As an analyst, I want one sheet per analyzed dimension named after the dimension.
Expected Behavior: Each results key becomes sheet via _build_unique_sheet_name (max 31 chars, numeric suffix on collision); DataFrame columns written as headers from row 3; dict results as key-value rows.

User Story: As a debug user, I want optional DataFrame sheets when metadata populated.
Expected Behavior: When non-empty: Peer Weights (weights_df), Weight Methods (method_breakdown_df), Privacy Validation (privacy_validation_df), Rank Changes, Subset Search, Structural Summary/Detail, Impact Detail/Summary, Preset Comparison, Secondary Metrics.

User Story: As a user disabling privacy sheets, I want Weight Methods retained without Privacy Validation.
Expected Behavior: output.include_privacy_validation=false omits Privacy Validation sheet; Weight Methods still written.

User Story: As a user with input validation, I want issues tabulated in Excel.
Expected Behavior: Sheet 'Data Quality': PASSED/ISSUES FOUND status, error/warn/info counts, columns Severity/Category/Message/Details(JSON)/Sample Rows; severity color fills; added when metadata.validation_issues present.

User Story: As an auditor, I want raw metadata key-values in a Metadata sheet.
Expected Behavior: Sheet 'Metadata' lists metadata keys; DataFrames shown as 'DataFrame rows=N cols=M'; lists/dicts JSON-serialized.

User Story: As a user running --analyze-distortion, I want aggregate distortion by dimension.
Expected Behavior: Sheet 'Distortion Summary' with headers Dimension, Mean (PP), Min (PP), Max (PP), Std Dev; title notes percentage-point impact.

User Story: As a user running --compare-presets, I want preset distortion comparison.
Expected Behavior: Sheet 'Preset Comparison': Preset, Mean Distortion (PP), Max Distortion (PP), Time (s), Best; best row highlighted green when Best='*'.

User Story: As a user without openpyxl, I want a clear install message.
Expected Behavior: _ensure_excel_support raises ImportError mentioning 'pip install openpyxl' and suggests csv/json formats.

User Story: As a security-conscious user, I want exported strings neutralized against Excel formula injection.
Expected Behavior: sanitize_cell prefixes strings starting with = + - @ tab CR with apostrophe; applied via _excel_safe_value in Excel and object columns in balanced CSV.

User Story: As a stakeholder, I want a simplified publication Excel without solver internals.
Expected Behavior: File {stem}_publication.xlsx: Executive Summary (entity/date/compliance fields), per-dimension formatted tables with blue headers/borders, auto column widths capped at 50.

User Story: As a rate stakeholder, I want fraud rates shown in basis points when configured.
Expected Behavior: When analysis_type=rate and fraud_in_bps=True: numeric rate columns matching should_convert_rate_column multiplied by 100; fraud column labels gain (bps); fraud-prefixed sheets rename Balanced Peer Average (%) etc.

User Story: As a publication recipient, I must not see peer composition in sensitive sheets.
Expected Behavior: _publication_safe_metadata redacts weights_df, privacy_validation_df, rank_changes_df to single-row placeholder 'WITHHELD - Control 3.3 - peer group composition is confidential'.

User Story: As a stakeholder, I want selected compliance evidence in publication output.
Expected Behavior: Publication includes Impact Summary, Peer Weights (redacted), Privacy Validation (redacted), Rank Changes (redacted), Preset Comparison; excludes Weight Methods, Subset Search, Structural Detail/Summary, Data Quality, Metadata.

User Story: As a user, I want analysis/publication/both output modes respected.
Expected Behavior: output_format analysis => analysis .xlsx only; publication => _publication.xlsx only; both => both files via OutputArtifactWriter.write_analysis/write_publication.

User Story: As a strict-compliance user, I want publication withheld when violations exist.
Expected Behavior: posture=strict and violations>0 sets publication_output=None, metadata.publication_withheld_reason='strict_posture_violations'; analysis workbook still written when format includes analysis.

User Story: As an integrator, I want machine-readable JSON beside the workbook when configured.
Expected Behavior: When output.format=json: writes {stem}.json with schema_version=1, analysis_type, generated_by=autobench, publication_safe=False, run_status, compliance_verdict, metadata (DataFrames as records), results.

User Story: As a rate integrator, I want nested rate results flattened in JSON.
Expected Behavior: Rate runs flatten results to keys '{rate_type}_{dimension}' in JSON and multi-rate Excel paths.

User Story: As a user exporting balanced data, I want a predictable CSV path.
Expected Behavior: export_balanced_csv writes {output_stem}_balanced.csv adjacent to the Excel output path.

User Story: As a share analyst, I want weighted peer totals per dimension-category.
Expected Behavior: Columns: Dimension, Category, optional time_col, Balanced_{metric} (rounded 2dp); secondary metrics use original column names; sorted Dimension, time, Category; peers exclude target entity.

User Story: As a share analyst, I want raw/balanced shares and impact when --include-calculated.
Expected Behavior: Adds Raw_{metric}, Raw_{metric}_Share_%, Balanced_{metric}_Share_%, {metric}_Impact_PP; peer-only skips target share calcs with log message.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want weighted peer totals per dimension-category.
Expected Behavior: Columns: Dimension, Category, optional time_col, dynamic total_col and numerator column names with balanced sums; peers exclude target; rounded 2dp.

User Story: As a rate analyst, I want raw/balanced rates and impact PP when --include-calculated.
Expected Behavior: Adds Raw_Total, Balanced_Total, Balanced_Approval_Total, Raw/Balanced_Approval_Rate_%, Approval_Impact_PP, and fraud equivalents when fraud numerator configured.

User Story: As internal tooling, I want a reusable balanced metrics table.
Expected Behavior: get_balanced_metrics_df returns Dimension, Category, optional time, Balanced_{metric}/Balanced_Total/Balanced_Approval_Total/Balanced_Fraud_Total columns using WeightLookup per dimension.

User Story: As a compliance user, I want balanced CSV checked against the workbook after export.
Expected Behavior: When output.validate_export=True (default): rate runs invoke utils/csv_validator.py (mode=full); share runs check Dimension, Category, Balanced_* schema (mode=schema); result stored metadata.export_validation; strict posture raises RuntimeError on failure.

User Story: As an auditor, I want to manually cross-check CSV balanced exports vs Excel rates/shares.
Expected Behavior: utils/csv_validator.py compares per-dimension category rates from CSV totals to Excel dimension sheets (skips Summary, Metadata, diagnostic sheets); default tolerance 0.0001; exit 0 pass / 1 fail.

User Story: As an auditor, I want a text audit log alongside reports.
Expected Behavior: Writes {stem}_audit.log via create_audit_log: ANALYSIS METADATA key-values (DataFrames compacted), RESULTS SUMMARY (dimensions, categories, impact, privacy rows, outputs, balanced_csv path, validation counts), timestamp.

User Story: As an auditor, I want a zip of run evidence.
Expected Behavior: write_audit_package creates {stem}_audit_package.zip containing report xlsx files, balanced CSV, audit log, config_snapshot.json (secrets redacted), validation_summary.json; skips missing files with warning.

User Story: As a security reviewer, I want secrets masked in config snapshots.
Expected Behavior: _redact_secrets replaces values for keys in {connection_string,pwd,password,uid,user,token,secret,api_key} with ***REDACTED*** recursively.

User Story: As the output pipeline, I want typed report models from analysis results.
Expected Behavior: build_analysis_artifacts creates AnalysisArtifacts with ReportModel (requires compliance_summary), publication path {stem}_publication.xlsx, and diagnostic DataFrames; ReportModel.to_metadata merges optional frames into legacy metadata keys.

User Story: As an analyst debugging LP infeasibility, I want structural cap diagnostics in Excel.
Expected Behavior: DiagnosticsEngine.compute_structural_caps_diagnostics produces Structural Detail (per peer/category margin_over_cap_pp) and Structural Summary (infeasible_categories, infeasible_peers, worst_margin_pp per dimension) written when non-empty.

User Story: As an analyst, I want subset search attempts logged in Excel.
Expected Behavior: Sheet 'Subset Search' written from metadata.subset_search_df when non-empty; includes Dimensions column per tests.

User Story: As an operator, I want timing events captured in run metadata.
Expected Behavior: RunObservability.record appends {event, elapsed_s, **kwargs}; as_metadata returns events list and total_elapsed_s included in analysis metadata.
Verification Method: Inspect Metadata sheet or JSON metadata.observability after run

User Story: As a user, I want CLI flags to override custom config files, which override presets, which override built-in defaults.
Expected Behavior: ConfigManager applies layers in order: _get_default_config() → _load_preset() → load_config(config_file) → _apply_cli_overrides(); later layers win on key conflicts via deep_merge.

User Story: As a user, I want a sensible LP tolerance when I do not specify a preset or override.
Expected Behavior: Default optimization.linear_programming.tolerance is 1.0 (percentage points) from _get_default_config().

User Story: As a user, I want --preset to change optimization settings without a custom config file.
Expected Behavior: ConfigManager(preset='compliance_strict') sets optimization.linear_programming.tolerance to 0.0 from presets/compliance_strict.yaml, overriding the 1.0 default.

User Story: As a user, I want --config to override values from a selected preset.
